_meta.policy: Robert is read-only. Only Tumelo (admin) may add or edit facts. Every fact requires source_url and last_verified_at.

tier_definitions.verified: Achieved. Assertable as fact in any document. Has source_url and last_verified_at within the last 12 months.
tier_definitions.target: Goal or projection. MUST appear with explicit 'target' / 'projected' / 'by [year]' labelling. Never as achieved.
tier_definitions.needs_confirmation: Known in flux. Sources conflict or fact is stale. Verifier MUST replace with placeholder + flag for Tumelo review.

source_url_conventions.PUBLIC: Real URL to an authoritative third-party source. Linkable in documents.
source_url_conventions.INTERNAL: Reference to a Blastbeat-internal document (curriculum spec, chapter ledger, etc.). Format: 'INTERNAL: <document-name> [<section>] (<owner>)'. Not linkable; Tumelo can produce on request.
source_url_conventions.PENDING: No source yet. Fact is provisional. Verifier treats as needs_confirmation regardless of declared tier.

_meta.review_cadence: Tumelo runs a facts-QA pass quarterly. Any fact older than 12 months auto-downgrades to needs_confirmation in the verifier.

aliases: more than a third of a million young people

verified.claim: ESE sprint runs one school term (~12 weeks)
verified.note: AMBIGUITY FLAGGED — original audit prompt said both '90 days' AND '16 weeks'. 90 days = ~12.8 weeks (one SA school term). 16 weeks = ~112 days (closer to a UK/IE half-year). Hypothesis A: the figure is ONE SCHOOL TERM ≈ 12 weeks. Hypothesis B: there are two distinct durations — sprint (12wk) and an extended track (16wk). Tumelo to pick before any document uses this fact; the verifier will surface this as needs_confirmation until Tumelo flips status_pending_human_confirmation to false.
verified.source_url: PENDING: curriculum spec section 2.1 — Tumelo to attach

tumelo_decisions.rationale: One SA school term, matches CAPS calendar
Extended track including pre-event prep + post-event reporting
Two distinct durations — needs separate facts (v.programme.duration.sprint and v.programme.duration.extended)

South Africa Phase 1: Western Cape, 12 schools, 504 enrolled students

verified.claim: Japan chapter: independent NPO operating 17 years (since 2009), ~900 alumni across 150+ high schools and 110 universities, 122 mini music companies, NPO-certified by the Japan Cabinet Office
verified.origin_story: July 2009: Takamasa Matsuura saw Robert Stephenson's work featured on NHK's 'Changemaker' programme and reached out to bring Blastbeat to Japan. August 2009: Robert visited, gave lectures at companies and schools, a volunteer-run secretariat was established. November 2009 – March 2010: first eight programmes ran with high-school and university students, including refugee-camp support with the UNHCR Association of Japan and a Japan–Korea bilateral mini-music-company programme with the Japan Foundation. Programmes ran in Tokyo, Okinawa, Kyoto and Aichi.
milestones: 2011: Seven Tokyo teams ran simultaneously; planning began for disaster-area programmes
2012: Second programmes in Tokyo, Fukushima, Kyoto; expanded to three cohorts per year
2013: First programmes in Ishikawa, Kanazawa, Miyagi (Ishinomaki) and Fukuoka; collaborations with El Sistema (Venezuela) and NPO Peace Boat
2014–2019: Sustained operation across Kanto, Okinawa, Ibaraki/Mito, Kanazawa, with growing cohort sizes
2025–2026: Active spring + autumn cohorts; 17 years operating
verified.programme_structure: 100-day programme, twice per year (spring April–May, autumn October–November), free for participants, volunteer-supported
verified.source_url_status: snippet_only_at_retrieval — JP sites returned 403 to automated fetch; numbers were verified via search-engine snippets quoting blastbeat.jp directly. Tumelo to confirm by direct page visit before any high-stakes proposal cites these figures.
verified.verification_note: Independent NPO with 17-year track record built largely without active contact with Blastbeat global founder Robert Stephenson since 2009. As of June 2026 the global team has re-discovered the Japan chapter and intends to re-establish friendly contact, with an open invitation (no expectation) for them to access the new V2 digital platform if useful. Until they confirm appetite for closer collaboration, public references should celebrate them as an INDEPENDENT chapter, not as a Blastbeat-managed outpost.

government_frameworks.official_name: National Development Plan 2030: Our Future — Make It Work
government_frameworks.blastbeat_mapping: BlastBeat ESE addresses NDP Chapter 9 (Improving education) through enterprise-skills curriculum integration, and Chapter 3 (Economy and employment) through real youth-led businesses generating actual revenue.

aspirations_relevant_to_blastbeat: Aspiration 1: Prosperous Africa based on inclusive growth
Aspiration 6: People-driven development relying on the potential of African people, especially women and youth

government_frameworks._comment: Stubs below — structure only. Verifier will REFUSE alignment to these until a human author + reviewer signs off.
